items.title: Heracles zet zich voor schut in Deventer: eerste Go Ahead-zege sinds begin november
items.summary: In Deventer viel zondag eindelijk weer eens iets te lachen. Lokale trots Go Ahead Eagles boekte tegen Heracles Almelo de eerste overwinning sinds 9 november 2025, toen er met 2-1 gewonnen werd van Feyenoord. Mede door geklungel van de bezoekers liepen de Eagles uit tot een riante zege. En dat geeft Kowet weer een klein beetje lucht in de strijd tegen degradatie.

items.title: Chelsea v Manchester United: Women’s FA Cup fifth round – live
items.summary: Updates on the 1.30pm (GMT) kick-off at Kingsmeadow Report: Birmingham 8-0 Chatham | Email Xaymaca here 5 min: Lauren James progresses with the ball before slipping in Thompson. Thompson plays it on to Johanna Rytting Kaneryd but she can’t get a shot away. 4 min: Chelsea are dominating possession in the opening stages. Continue reading...

items.title: Nottingham Forest v Liverpool: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 2pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Follow us on Bluesky | And email John Eric Peterson gets in touch: “I wouldn’t mind Wayne Rooney pulling on an old Everton kit and getting on some podcast to remind Arne Slot, “Easy there, sport. You say that the only thing you and Jurgen Klopp have in common is that you both won the league. That’s not true. You both won the league with Jurgen’s team. Whether you can build a champion of your own is a different question.” Arne Slot just spoke to Sky, starting with Dominik Szoboszlai at full-back: “He needs to be because that’s what we need. We have our issues, especially in defence. Missing our 2 fullbacks, but Dominic has done that job really well. Last week, Curtis Jones, did his job really well. So that’s the good thing about midfielders, they are usually able to play in more positions than only in the midfields. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Go Ahead fileert Heracles, PEC maakt gelijk in Utrecht
items.summary: Zondag staan er liefst vijf Eredivisie-duels op het programma. De wedstrijd van Feyenoord volg je in een apart liveblog, voor de rest kun je in deze VI Live terecht, net als voor al het buitenlandse voetbal.

items.title: Van den Brom onder de indruk van Vaessen: 'Je speelt tegen twaalf man'
items.summary: Etienne Vaessen kan John van den Brom wel bekoren. De doelman ging met FC Groningen weliswaar onderuit bij het FC Twente van Van den Brom, maar na afloop was de trainer vol lof over zijn tegenstander. 'Hij kan zo verschrikkelijk goed voetballen.'

items.title: Jarvis wins first DP World Tour title at Kenya Open
items.summary: South African golfer Casey Jarvis won the Kenya Open by three shots on Sunday to clinch his first title on the DP World Tour.

items.title: Sheffield Wednesday endure historic relegation from Championship after derby defeat
items.summary: Sheffield Wednesday’s three-year stay in the Championship was ended in a cruel final twist of fate by Sheffield United after a 2-1 derby defeat at Bramall Lane. For their city rivals to operate the relegation trap door only added insult to injury as Wednesday’s miserable mathematical fate was confirmed. Continue reading...

items.title: Doek valt voor Sheffield Wednesday: snelste degradant ooit in Engels profvoetbal
items.summary: Sheffield Wednesday is gedegradeerd. Uitgerekend stadsgenoot Sheffield United gaf zondagmiddag het laatste zetje richting League One. Nooit eerder daalde een club in februari af in het Engelse profvoetbal.

items.title: Memphis stipt verschil tussen Brazilië en Europa aan: 'Meer met het hart'
items.summary: Memphis Depay speelt alweer anderhalf jaar voor Corinthians en inmiddels kan de Oranje-international dan ook een aardig woordje meepraten over de verschillen tussen Brazilië en Europa. Die zijn groot, volgens de spits, die het in São Paulo goed naar zijn zin heeft en ervoor openstaat om zijn aflopende contract te verlengen.

items.title: 'Unforeseen circumstances' delay Aberdeen boss hunt
items.summary: Aberdeen's search for a new head coach has been delayed by "unforeseen circumstances", with chairman Dave Cormack stressing the club "mustn't rush or compromise" an appointment.

items.title: Pep recommends cocktails after 'massive' City win
items.summary: Pep Guardiola hailed a "massive" win after title-chasing Manchester City held on for a crucial yet nervy victory over Newcastle on Saturday.

items.title: Arteta: Players must accept 'noise' in title race
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has told his title-chasing players to join another club if they can't handle the "noise and bullets" that comes with fighting for the Premier League.

items.title: Klopp: Liverpool success impossible without Milner
items.summary: Jürgen Klopp has said that James Milner's achievement of breaking the Premier League appearance record is unlikely to ever be broken, and praised him for his Liverpool input.

items.title: LAFC down Miami in season opener at Coliseum
items.summary: Inter Miami's defense of its MLS Cup title began in disappointing fashion, with a 3-0 defeat to LAFC at the LA Coliseum on Saturday.

items.title: Burnley, Chelsea condemn racist abuse of players
items.summary: Burnley and Chelsea have condemned online racist abuse directed at their respective players, Hannibal Mejbri and Wesley Fofana, following Saturday's 1-1 draw between the teams.

items.title: Palace latest: 'Board inept, Glasner finished' - banner displays fan fury
Six Nations LIVE! Bielle-Biarrey scores again as France strike early vs Italy
Guardiola tells team to chill with cocktails as Man City pile pressure on Arsenal
items.summary: As Manchester City continue to crank up the pressure on Arsenal, Pep Guardiola tells his players to take three days off to "enjoy life" with "caipirinhas and daiquiris".

items.title: ‘Caipirinhas, daiquiris’: Guardiola tells City players to chill and ignore title pressure
items.summary: Manchester City beat Newcastle to cut Arsenal’s lead to two Team given three days off before facing Leeds Pep Guardiola told his players to “take a lot of caipirinhas, daiquiris” to handle the pressure of the title chase after Manchester City beat Newcastle 2-1 to close to within two points of Arsenal. Two goals from Nico O’Reilly on 14 and 27 minutes at the Etihad Stadium were answered only by Lewis Hall’s strike in between as City moved to 56 points and a goal difference of 31, one fewer than Arsenal’s. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Educate yourself and your kids' - Fofana and Mejbri racially abused
items.summary: Burnley midfielder Hannibal Mejbri and Chelsea defender Wesley Fofana have spoken out on social media after being sent racist abuse in the aftermath of the clubs' Premier League match on Saturday.
